headline: Retail investors fuel record buying in US software ETF as AI trade broadens
summary: The largest exchange-traded fund (ETF) tracking U.S. software stocks clocked the biggest single-day buy-in by retail investors ‌on record on Monday, data from Vanda Research showed. Software ‌stocks have largely recovered their losses from earlier this year when fears of ​industry-wide disruptions due to the rise of AI gripped the sector, though volatility still persists. Mom-and-pop traders bought stock worth a net $46 million in the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF on Monday, surpassing ‌the previous single-day ⁠record by roughly 40%, as per Vanda.

content.title: Retail investors fuel record buying in US software ETF as AI trade broadens
content.summary: The largest exchange-traded fund (ETF) tracking U.S. software stocks clocked the biggest single-day buy-in by retail investors ‌on record on Monday, data from Vanda Research showed. Mom-and-pop traders bought stock worth a net $46 million in the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF on Monday, surpassing ‌the previous single-day ⁠record by roughly 40%, as per Vanda.

headline: Software stocks whipsaw amid AI fears, as Anthropic IPO looms
summary: The software sector is currently caught in a tug-of-war between profound optimism and existential dread over the future of artificial intelligence. After a brief period of euphoria, reality—and algorithmic trading—bit back hard, sending major software stocks tumbling in a sudden reversal. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF fell 3%, wiping out half of the 6% gains it had recorded just a day prior. The bleeding was widespread across the industry’s biggest names: Atlassian plunged 8%, Hu

headline: #StandWithOwners is back: TELUS continues its commitment to boosting Canadian small business and strengthening the business owner community
summary: TELUS' #StandWithOwners contest is returning for its seventh consecutive year to boost Canadian small businesses. Passionately committed to supporting business owners across Canada, TELUS has invested close to $7 million since 2020 to #StandWithOwners, providing funding, technology, and exposure to help small businesses thrive in a digital world.

headline: Is Zoom Stock A Buy After The Anthropic IPO Hype?
summary: Zoom (ZM) is actively shedding its reputation as a single-use utility. Following a robust Q1 FY27 earnings beat and a speculative surge tied to Anthropic's IPO plans, shares have rallied to $112. As the stock trades at a notable premium, investors must determine if Zoom's AI-driven enterprise pivot truly justifies this momentum.

headline: Salesforce AI Push With Anthropic And Contentful Meets Undervalued Share Price
summary: Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) has announced a $5b investment in AI company Anthropic in order to deepen AI capabilities across its platform. The company also agreed to acquire Contentful, a widely used content platform, to strengthen the content layer of its Customer 360 offering. These moves come as Salesforce focuses on AI driven customer experiences and broader platform extensibility. For investors watching NYSE:CRM, these announcements arrive with the stock at $209.6 and a mixed return profile...
